foundation.title: ARRAY
foundation.subtitle: Fixed Row of Data
foundation.content: An array is a fixed-size row of items, all of the same type. In C, you must tell the compiler exactly how many items (size) you will store when you create the array.
foundation.scenario: A Row of 10 School Lockers. You can't add an 11th locker later (Fixed Size). You can only put 'Books' (Type) inside if the lockers are for books.

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Asking for 5 integer slots in RAM instantly.

examples.title: Initialization
examples.explanation: Filling the slots with starting values at birth.

examples.title: Zero Init
examples.explanation: Starting every slot at 0 to avoid garbage memory values.

foundation.keyPoint: Fixed size at creation. Elements are side-by-side in RAM.

foundation.title: INDEX
foundation.subtitle: Accessing Doors
foundation.content: You access elements using their index []. In C, indexing starts at 0. The first item is at index 0, the second at 1, and so on.
foundation.scenario: A Staircase. The Ground Floor is Step 0. To reach the first floor, you take Step 1, then 2. You can't go to Step 3 if the stairs only have 2 steps (Crash).

examples.title: Read Data
examples.explanation: Retrieving a value from the third locker (Index 2).

examples.title: Write Data
examples.explanation: Changing the value stored in the first locker (Index 0).

examples.title: The Bounds
examples.explanation: Accessing index 5 when the size is ONLY 5 will crash with a 'Seg Fault'.

foundation.title: STRINGS
foundation.subtitle: Char Arrays
foundation.content: C doesn't have a 'String' type. It uses an array of characters (char) that must end with a Null Terminator '\0' to signal the end.
foundation.scenario: A Sentence with a Period. A string is a list of letters. Without the period (Null Terminator), a computer will keep reading the blank wall behind the paper forever.

examples.title: Literal
examples.explanation: C adds the '\0' automatically if you use double quotes.

examples.title: Manual Array
examples.explanation: Building a string letter by letter, including the terminator.

examples.title: Length Check
examples.explanation: Counting letters until the char equals '\0'.

foundation.keyPoint: Strings in C are char arrays ending in \0.

foundation.title: MEMORY
foundation.subtitle: Linear RAM
foundation.content: In C, array elements are contiguous (touching) in memory. This is why C is so fast—the CPU can jump directly between them.
foundation.scenario: A Train Track. Each carriage (Variable) is coupled tightly to the next. You don't jump gaps; the data flows in a straight, solid line.

examples.title: Pointer Math
examples.explanation: Adding 1 to an address jumps to the NEXT array slot.

examples.title: Sizeof Logic
examples.explanation: Calculating how many total bytes the whole array takes in RAM.

examples.title: Zero Gap
examples.explanation: C guarantees there is NO wasted space between array items.

gameData.q: What is the first index of any C array?
